title: Scorpion scorpion_exo_1400_air_course-tc-2

specs.material: Carbon Fiber Composite Shell with Polycarbonate Liner
specs.strap_type: Quick-Release Magnetic Buckle with YKK Hardware

technical_analysis: The scorpion_exo_1400_air_course-tc-2 utilizes a proprietary carbon fiber weave architecture that significantly reduces mass without compromising structural integrity. This material choice allows the helmet to achieve an industry-leading weight of 1280 grams while maintaining high impact resistance standards required by ECE 22.06 and DOT FMVSS 218 certifications. The internal liner system features a multi-density EPS foam configuration designed to dissipate kinetic energy across a broader surface area, reducing focal points of stress during an impact event.
technical_analysis_2: Aerodynamic optimization was central to the design phase, resulting in a streamlined shell profile that minimizes turbulent airflow at high velocities. The integrated ventilation channels are engineered to create a pressure differential that actively pulls cool air through the intake vents and expels hot air from rear exhausts, achieving a ventilation efficiency score of 92 out of 100. This active cooling system is critical for maintaining rider focus during extended high-speed sessions where thermal regulation is paramount.
technical_analysis_3: The acoustic profile has been tuned to balance wind noise reduction with communication clarity. By utilizing internal acoustic dampening layers and a smooth chin bar contour, the helmet reduces wind noise to 78.5 dB at 100 km/h, which is competitive within its class. The magnetic quick-release strap system ensures rapid donning and doffing while providing a secure fit that accommodates various head shapes through the intermediate oval geometry, ensuring consistent retention during aggressive cornering maneuvers.
